meta.summary: ByteDance Seed introduced Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM, a new voice model designed for real-time, interruption-tolerant conversation with attentive listening and interference suppression, alongside a Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview release on Arena. Google expanded its AI-powered Google Finance experience to Europe, extending generative financial search and analysis into a major new market. On the research side, the paper "Tool Calling is Linearly Readable and Steerable in Language Models" reports that tool selection signals are linearly decodable across 12 instruction-tuned models from 270M to 27B parameters, pointing to a potentially practical control layer for agent reliability.

items.title: Future Validity is the Missing Statistic: From Impossibility to $\Phi$-Estimation for Grammar-Faithful Speculative Decoding
items.summary_short: Grammar-constrained generation is often combined with local vocabulary masking and speculative decoding, but the resulting sampling law is not the grammar-conditional distribution users usually intend.

items.title: Beyond LoRA vs. Full Fine-Tuning: Gradient-Guided Optimizer Routing for LLM Adaptation
items.summary_short: Recent literature on fine-tuning Large Language Models highlights a fundamental debate. While Full Fine-Tuning (FFT) provides the representational plasticity required for high-entropy knowledge injection, Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) can match or surpass FFT performance because many tasks only

topic_refs.headline: Beyond LoRA vs. Full Fine-Tuning: Gradient-Guided Optimizer Routing for LLM Adaptation

items.title: A Rod Flow Model for Adam at the Edge of Stability
items.summary_short: Cohen et al. (arXiv:2207.14484) observed that adaptive gradient methods such as Adam operate at the edge of stability. While there has been significant work on continuous-time modeling of gradient descent at the edge of stability, extending these models to momentum methods remains underdeveloped.

topic_refs.headline: A Rod Flow Model for Adam at the Edge of Stability

items.title: Where's the Plan? Locating Latent Planning in Language Models with Lightweight Mechanistic Interventions
items.summary_short: We study planning site formation in language models -- where internal representations of structurally-constrained future tokens form during the forward pass, and whether they causally drive generation.

topic_refs.headline: Where's the Plan? Locating Latent Planning in Language Models with Lightweight Mechanistic Interventions

items.title: The Coupling Tax: How Shared Token Budgets Undermine Visible Chain-of-Thought Under Fixed Output Limits
items.summary_short: Chain-of-thought reasoning is often treated as a monotone way to improve language-model accuracy by letting a model think longer.

topic_refs.headline: The Coupling Tax: How Shared Token Budgets Undermine Visible Chain-of-Thought Under Fixed Output Limits

items.title: Tree SAE: Learning Hierarchical Feature Structures in Sparse Autoencoders
items.summary_short: Learning hierarchical features in Sparse Autoencoders (SAEs) is essential for capturing the structured nature of real-world data and mitigating issues like feature absorption or splitting.

items.title: Toeplitz MLP Mixers are Low Complexity, Information-Rich Sequence Models
items.summary_short: Transformer-based large language models are in some respects limited by the quadratic time and space computational complexity of attention.

items.title: Star Elastic: Many-in-One Reasoning LLMs with Efficient Budget Control
items.summary_short: Training a family of large language models (LLMs), either from scratch or via iterative compression, is prohibitively expensive and inefficient, requiring separate training runs for each model in the family.

items.title: Self-Programmed Execution for Language-Model Agents
items.summary_short: At the heart of existing language model agents is a fixed orchestrator program responsible for the state transition between consecutive turns.

items.title: Revisiting Transformer Layer Parameterization Through Causal Energy Minimization
items.summary_short: Transformer blocks typically combine multi-head attention (MHA) for token mixing with gated MLPs for token-wise feature transformation, yet many choices in their parameterization remain largely empirical.

items.title: Rethinking State Tracking in Recurrent Models Through Error Control Dynamics
items.summary_short: The theory of state tracking in recurrent architectures has predominantly focused on expressive capacity: whether a fixed architecture can theoretically realize a set of symbolic transition rules.

featured_topics.headline: ByteDance Seed Ships Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM
featured_topics.summary_short: ByteDance Seed introduced its Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM. The announcement highlights attentive listening, interference suppression, and more natural conversational interaction
featured_topics.why_it_matters: ByteDance is signaling continued investment in real-time multimodal interfaces, a strategically important category for assistants, wearables, and customer-service automation.
key_takeaways: Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM is positioned around real-time conversational behavior, including attentive listening, interruption handling, and robust interference suppression rather than simple turn-based voice I/O.
The same ByteDance Seed update stream also announced Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview on Arena, suggesting the company is iterating both speech and general model capabilities in parallel.
The announcement came directly from ByteDance Seed, making this an official product artifact rather than a third-party leak or benchmark claim.

evidence.title: ByteDance Seed 2027 Foundation Model Campus Recruitment Open
Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview Model Release on Arena

featured_topics.headline: Google Expands AI-Powered Google Finance to Europe
featured_topics.summary_short: Google announced that its AI-powered Google Finance experience is expanding to Europe. The update extends an existing finance product feature to additional markets
featured_topics.why_it_matters: Google is embedding generative AI deeper into high-intent financial workflows, which matters for distribution, monetization, and competitive pressure on fintech research products.
key_takeaways: The rollout extends the new AI-powered Google Finance experience beyond its initial market into Europe, increasing the reachable user base for AI-assisted investing and market research.
The artifact is a live product expansion from Google AI Blog, not a concept demo, indicating operational confidence in the feature set.
Because Google Finance sits inside a broader Google ecosystem, the European launch strengthens Google's position in consumer financial discovery against standalone finance and data platforms.

evidence.title: The new AI-powered Google Finance is expanding to Europe.

featured_topics.headline: "Tool Calling is Linearly Readable and Steerable in Language Models" Finds Decodable Tool-Choice Signals Across 12 Models
featured_topics.summary_short: When a tool-calling agent picks the wrong tool, the failure is invisible until execution: the email gets sent, the meeting gets missed.
featured_topics.why_it_matters: If tool selection can be read and steered with lightweight probes, enterprises may gain a lower-cost path to improving agent reliability without retraining frontier models.
key_takeaways: The paper studies 12 instruction-tuned models spanning Gemma 3, Qwen 3, Qwen 2.5, and Llama 3.1, with parameter sizes ranging from 270M to 27B.
Authors report that the identity of the chosen tool is linearly readable from model representations, implying tool-use decisions are encoded in a relatively accessible form before execution.
The work also claims steerability, suggesting interventions on internal representations can shift tool choice and potentially reduce silent agent failures such as wrong API calls or missed actions.

evidence.title: Tool Calling is Linearly Readable and Steerable in Language Models
